description: Figure 7 presents a comprehensive mechanical property comparison of five low-density nano-bainitic Al-C steels (S1-S5) subjected to various austenitization (T_A) and austempering (T_B) conditions. The multi-axis plot displays ultimate tensile strength UTS (black squares, left axis), yield strength Rp0.2 (blue downward triangles, right axis), elongation El (red triangles, right axis in percent), and hardness (black circles, left axis with HV30 values labeled below each condition ranging from 443 to 500). Evaluated conditions span T_A = 1150-1220 °C and T_B = 270-365 °C. S1 and S2 attain peak UTS values near 1600 MPa at lower T_B (300 °C) but with reduced ductility. S3 requires an elevated T_A of 1220 °C due to high Al content and shows the lowest elongation at 270 °C. Notably, S4 exhibits the best strength-ductility balance, achieving approximately 10% elongation with a maximum reaching 12% per caption at T_A = 1150 °C and T_B = 350 °C while maintaining UTS near 1360 MPa. Hardness remains comparatively stable across alloys and treatments (~450-500 HV30). The figure demonstrates that alloy design and austempering temperature critically govern the strength-elongation trade-off in nano-bainitic steels, with Ti-microalloyed S4 offering superior mechanical property synergy.
